title: Redesign ProcessingScreen layout with logo, animated progress bars with % counter, and simplified upload

requirements.summary: Redesign ProcessingScreen layout to match reference design with logo placement, subtitle, circular status icons, and footer disclaimer
acceptanceCriteria: App logo appears above the 'Analisando Gráfico' title
Subtitle 'Detectando padrões de velas localmente...' appears below the title
Four stage rows are displayed in a dark card/panel
Completed stages have filled green circle checkmark icons and green text
Active stage has spinning ring icon and white bold text
Pending stages have dim/gray circle icons and gray text
Footer 'Análise local — sem envio de dados externos' is visible at the bottom

file_operations.description: Restructure the page layout to place the generated app logo (frontend/public/assets/generated/app-icon.dim_512x512.png) above the 'Analisando Gráfico' heading, add subtitle 'Detectando padrões de velas localmente...' below the heading, wrap all four processing stages in a dark elevated card container, and add footer text 'Análise local — sem envio de dados externos' at the bottom. Replace existing horizontal progress bars with circular status icons: pending stages show dim gray circles, active stage shows a spinning ring loader, completed stages show filled green checkmark circles. Update stage text colors: completed in green, active in white/bold, pending in gray. Maintain all existing error handling logic and stage progression.

Replace the current status indicator implementation with circular icon variants. For pending state: render a dim gray outlined circle. For active state: render a spinning ring loader animation (CSS-based or SVG spinner). For complete state: render a filled green circle with a white checkmark icon inside. Update text styling: completed stages use green color, active stage uses white bold text, pending stages use gray text. Remove any horizontal progress bar elements from this component.

requirements.summary: Replace stage status indicators with animated horizontal loading bars in black and white with glow effect and live percentage counter
acceptanceCriteria: Each stage row contains an animated horizontal progress bar
Active stage bar animates smoothly from 0% to 100%
A small percentage label (e.g., '73%') is displayed beside the bar and updates in real time
Bar fill color is white with a visible glow/shine CSS effect
Bar track background is dark/black
Completed stage bars remain filled at 100% in white
Pending stage bars are shown as dark empty tracks

file_operations.description: Replace the circular status icons implemented in REQ-107 with horizontal animated loading bars. Each bar consists of a black/dark track background and a white fill that animates from 0% to 100% width when the stage is active. Apply a CSS glow effect (box-shadow or filter: brightness) to the white fill for a shining appearance. Display a small percentage counter (e.g., '47%') beside the right end of each bar, updating in real-time as the bar fills. Pending bars render as empty dark tracks. Completed bars remain filled at 100% in white. Add state and useEffect logic to animate the percentage from 0 to 100 over the stage duration.

Update stage state management to pass current progress percentage (0-100) to each ProcessingStage component. Ensure the active stage receives animated percentage updates during its execution. Adjust stage timing logic if needed to support smooth percentage animation (e.g., use setInterval or animation frame callbacks to increment percentage state). Ensure completed stages pass 100% and pending stages pass 0%.

requirements.summary: Simplify AnalyzeChart page upload interface to accept any image input method without format restrictions
acceptanceCriteria: File picker accepts PNG, JPG, JPEG, WebP, and screenshot file types
Camera capture option is available
Clipboard paste (Ctrl+V) works to capture a copied screenshot
Drag-and-drop upload is supported
No format rejection errors appear for common screenshot files
Upload UI is minimal with Portuguese labels only

file_operations.description: Simplify the upload interface and remove all file format rejection logic that blocks common screenshot formats. Ensure the file picker accepts any image MIME type including image/png, image/jpeg, image/jpg, image/webp, and any screenshot file type. Integrate camera capture using the camera component (verify the component's usage instructions before implementing). Ensure clipboard paste (useClipboard hook) works for copied screenshots. Verify drag-and-drop via FileUpload component accepts all image formats. Simplify UI text to minimal Portuguese labels without verbose instructions. Remove any HEIC/HEIF rejection toasts or validation that would block user images.

Remove or relax file format validation logic that currently rejects HEIC/HEIF files. Update the accept attribute and validation to accept any common image MIME type (image/png, image/jpeg, image/jpg, image/webp, image/heic, image/heif). Remove error toasts for file format rejection. Keep the 10MB size limit but ensure all image formats are accepted. Simplify drag-and-drop UI text to minimal Portuguese.

Ensure the camera capture component integrates seamlessly into the AnalyzeChart page. Verify the useCamera hook is used correctly (check camera/useCamera.ts usage instructions). Ensure all UI text remains in Portuguese. Confirm camera preview has stable non-zero dimensions and aspect ratio. Ensure capture button is only clickable when camera is fully initialized. Display camera errors gracefully. Ensure captured photo is returned as a File and passed to the upload flow.

requirements.summary: Display uploaded chart image as a minimized thumbnail in Results page corner
acceptanceCriteria: Chart thumbnail is rendered at approximately 80×60px
Thumbnail is positioned in the corner of the results layout (not dominating the view)
Image is retrieved from sessionStorage 'chartImage' key
Thumbnail has a subtle border and rounded corners
Thumbnail respects the active dark/light theme

file_operations.description: Add a minimized chart thumbnail to the Results page layout. Retrieve the chart image data URL from sessionStorage using the 'chartImage' key. Render the thumbnail at approximately 80×60px size in a corner of the layout (top-right or bottom-right, whichever fits the existing design better). Apply subtle border and rounded corners (e.g., rounded-md border border-border). Ensure the thumbnail styling respects the active theme using Tailwind theme tokens (e.g., border-border, bg-card). Position the thumbnail absolutely or within a flex/grid layout so it does not dominate the results view. Do not modify the main results content layout or existing signal cards.

requirements.summary: Apply professional premium visual polish to ProcessingScreen with dark background, large bold typography, and elevated card styling
acceptanceCriteria: ProcessingScreen background is fully dark (black or near-black)
Heading typography is large and bold
Stage list is contained in a dark elevated card panel
Spacing and proportions feel premium and professional
All text labels are in Portuguese (Brazil)

file_operations.description: Apply premium visual polish to the ProcessingScreen. Set the page background to fully dark (bg-black or bg-zinc-950). Increase the 'Analisando Gráfico' heading to large bold typography (text-4xl or text-5xl font-bold). Wrap the stage list in a dark elevated card with subtle shadow and border (bg-card border border-border shadow-lg rounded-lg). Adjust spacing and padding for professional proportions (generous padding around heading, subtitle, and stage card; consistent internal padding in the card). Ensure all text remains in Portuguese (Brazil). Use Tailwind theme tokens for colors to respect dark/light theme.

If additional global CSS adjustments are needed for premium typography or spacing on the ProcessingScreen (e.g., custom heading line-height, letter-spacing, or glow effects for the loading bars), add them here using the existing CSS custom properties. Ensure changes are scoped or use utility classes to avoid affecting other pages. Keep all theme tokens consistent with the existing light/dark mode definitions.
